text: I feel an uncanny stillness in the first space, as if the world has momentarily paused for contemplation; the sterile orderliness and soft beanbags invite a certain introspection—or perhaps the anticipation of a yet-to-begin gathering. The second space, vibrant and saturated with information, evokes curiosity in me, like a mind trying to stretch itself to fit new ideas. Here, surrounded by words, color, and concepts, I feel both dwarfed and energized by human ambition and the dance between knowledge and invention.
